title: Intraday time-series momentum extends to commodity futures; stronger on high early volume/volatility (Jin-Kearney-Li-Yang)
claim: In commodity futures (copper, steel, soybean, soybean meal), the first half-hour return positively predicts the last half-hour return; in metals the effect strengthens when the early session has elevated volume or volatility, and a strategy trading the last half-hour in the first half-hour's direction beats buy-and-hold.

source.label: Jin, Kearney, Li & Yang (2019), 'Intraday time-series momentum: Evidence from China', Journal of Futures Markets 40(4), 632-650
source.quote: This study conducts an investigation of intraday time-series momentum across four Chinese commodity futures contracts: copper, steel, soybean, and soybean meal. Our results indicate that the first half-hour return positively predicts the last half-hour return across all four futures.

rule_formal: r_last = a + b*r_first + e per contract, b > 0 for all four; interaction: b larger conditional on high first-half-hour volume or volatility (metals). Trade rule: position in last half-hour = sign(r_first).

numbers.sample: Four Chinese commodity futures (copper, steel, soybean, soybean meal); sample period not stated on landing page
numbers.caveats: Coefficients/R-squared in full text only; Chinese sessions have lunch breaks and (pre-2013 metals) no night session — session mapping to 23h gold differs materially

mechanism: Day traders provide liquidity against the early informed move and must close positions before the session end to avoid overnight risk, forcing late-day flow in the direction of the early move — the same inventory-unwind channel proposed for equity ETFs, now shown in metals
relevance: Q5/Q1: confirms intraday momentum is not an equity-only artifact — it survives in metal futures, the closest asset class to gold found with a fetchable academic test; the volume/volatility interaction repeats the Q2 'works when participation is abnormal' condition; supports testing early-window direction as EOD-continuation conditioner on XAUUSD

test_design.lookahead_risk: First-window return and its volume/volatility known in realtime; for gold must choose the analog windows ex ante (e.g. first hour after London or NY open vs final hour before 23:00 EOD close)

overlap_risk: Volume/volatility interaction overlaps with dead rv variable at day level, but the conditioning here is same-day early-session (not prev-day) and the core signal (signed early return) was never among the five falsified variables
